verdict.answer_first: A states it immediately: "The main problem is the unguarded property access"; B also starts well but slightly broadens: "Yes, a few things"
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one clear safer version: "return u?.profile?.name ?? null" while naming the one design choice; B expands into multiple options and ends with "Want me to look..."
verdict.fabrication: B overstates context with "will actually throw in production" and leans on a future handoff; A: none
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it identifies the real bug, gives the fix, and states when not to add catch: "letting db.find rejections propagate... is usually correct"
